stmt: ∃ (α : Type*), ACR α ∧ ACR.Prov α ∧ ACR.Reft α ∧ ACR.APS α ∧ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)

For every `α` with `ACR α` and `ACR.Reft α`, `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` is equivalent to `∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g`, and hence `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤` is exactly the condition that `⊠⊤` itself is a Gödel fixed point.

If β carries a preorder with distinguished elements ⊤ and ⊥, an order-preserving operator □ with ∀ x, ¬ (□x ≤ x), and a point c with ⊤ ≤ c and ¬ (⊤ ≤ ⊥), then there is an APS expansion on β with ⊠x = c for all x such that ACR.Consistent β ∧ (⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint β)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)

Characterize exactly when a constant reflection operator can extend a given `ACR` and `Prov` structure to an `APS`: for fixed `c`, give necessary and sufficient conditions for the existence of `Reft` and `APS` with `∀ x, ⊠x = c`.
In equality-preorder `ACR` models with monotone `□`, determine whether the existence of a constant-reflection `APS` is equivalent to the existence of some `c` with `c = □c`.

∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) g.1)))

∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y})
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → ¬⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)

∀ {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) → (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ m : ℕ, Fintype.card α = 2 * m + 1)

∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n

For every cardinality `κ` with `κ ≠ 1`, there exists a type `α` of size `κ` carrying a consistent `ACR`/`Prov`/`Reft`/`APS`/`C5` structure such that `∀ x, x ≐ □x`, the order on `α` is total and antisymmetric, and `ACR.GödelFixpoint α` is nonempty.
